# Dogleg Data

> Golf's conventional wisdom, tested against real data. Sourced analysis for the 8-to-30 handicap.

Dogleg Data is an independent golf analytics publication for the golfer who plays a $65 muni and gets advice built from tour numbers. Every claim traces to a named source: Broadie's ShotLink data, Stagner and Arccos amateur numbers, USGA and R&A rough studies, GOLFTEC fitting data. Where a figure is extrapolated rather than pulled straight from a source, the chart labels it modeled.

## Pages

- [Home](https://doglegdata.com/): Site overview and the latest analysis, "Fairway vs. Rough, by Handicap."
- [Fairway vs. Rough, by Handicap](https://doglegdata.com/fairway-vs-rough/): The fairway is worth 75 yards to a tour pro at 150 out, and 17 to a 20-handicap, with sourced data you can check yourself.
- [Tools](https://doglegdata.com/fairway-vs-rough/dashboard.html): A scramble decision calculator, break-even explorer, and Monte Carlo GIR simulator, built on the same dataset as the post.
- [About](https://doglegdata.com/about/): Who runs Dogleg Data and the verification standard behind every analysis.
